name: Research Alliance Corp IV
business: Research Alliance Corporation IV is a blank check company incorporated on April 1, 2026, as a Cayman Islands exempted company for the purpose of effecting a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization or similar business combination with one or more businesses. The company has not selected any specific business combination target and has generated no operating revenues. It intends to focus on businesses in the healthcare industry, particularly in drug development, diagnostics, and healthcare technology.

exposures.sentence: In particular, we are targeting companies in the drug development and commercialization, diagnostic and healthcare technology and service sectors where our management has extensive investment experience.

RA Capital Management's investment activity is focused on identifying both private and public companies in the healthcare and life science sectors that are developing drugs, medical devices, and diagnostics.

In particular, we are targeting companies in the drug development and commercialization, diagnostic and healthcare technology and service sectors where our management has extensive investment experience.

In particular, we are targeting companies in the drug development and commercialization, diagnostic and healthcare technology and service sectors where our management has extensive investment experience.

catalysts.event: Must consummate initial business combination within 24 months from closing of offering.
catalysts.sentence: We will have up to 24 months from the closing of this offering to consummate an initial business combination.

tamClaims.claim: Total U.S. national health expenditures in 2024 were $5.3 trillion, accounting for 18% of U.S. GDP.
tamClaims.sentence: Overall, total U.S. national health expenditures grew to $5.3 trillion in 2024 and accounted for 18% of U.S Gross Domestic Product.

tamClaims.claim: Since 2020, there have been 216 M&A transactions totaling $534.0 billion in the biotechnology sector.
tamClaims.sentence: the past decade has seen a shift in the research and development strategy of the large pharma companies to external innovation through licensing partnerships and strategic acquisitions, resulting in 216 M&A transactions totaling $534.0 billion since 2020 according to Evaluate.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately $300.0 billion in annual revenue is at risk from loss of exclusivity in the 2026-2031 period for large pharma companies.
tamClaims.sentence: Across large pharma companies there is approximately $300.0 billion in annual revenue at risk from loss of exclusivity in 2026-2031 period, making the imperative to acquire greater than ever.
